_DOCUMENTATION._purpose: This config controls the table_maker system's AI behavior across 4 phases: Interview, Column Definition, Row Discovery, and QC Review
_DOCUMENTATION._usage: Loaded by execution.py and passed to respective handlers
_DOCUMENTATION._version: v2.2 - Progressive Escalation with Global Counter

interview._comment: Interview phase - AI interacts with user to understand requirements and approve table structure

background_research._comment: Step 0 - ALWAYS runs before column definition. Finds authoritative sources and starting tables to guide column design. On restructure, cached research is ALWAYS reused (never re-run)
background_research._note_model: Recommended: sonar-pro (fast, cost-effective), claude-sonnet-4-5 (highest quality). For complete enumeration, user should paste document text in interview.
background_research._note_max_tokens: Auto-tripled to 24000 when COMPLETE ENUMERATION signal detected (large entity lists need more output space)
background_research._web_search_params_info: Web search parameters differ by model family:
background_research._note_search_context: FOR PERPLEXITY MODELS ONLY (sonar, sonar-pro). Options: low/medium/high. High = comprehensive research
background_research._note_max_web_searches: FOR ANTHROPIC MODELS ONLY (claude-*). Number of web searches Claude can perform (if model=claude-sonnet-4-5)
background_research._note_min_tables: Minimum number of starting tables with sample entities required before proceeding to column definition
background_research._note_min_entities: Each starting table must include at least this many actual sample entities (not just URLs)

table_extraction._comment: Step 0b - Sequential follow-up to background research. Extracts complete tables from identified URLs. Only runs if background research identifies tables with extract_table=true
table_extraction._note_model: Recommended: sonar (fast extraction). Uses site-specific search via include_domains
table_extraction._note_max_tokens: Higher than background research to accommodate large tables with many rows
table_extraction._note_search_context: High context ensures complete table extraction including paginated content
table_extraction._note_timeout: Extraction timeout in seconds. Large tables may need more time to process

column_definition._comment: Step 1 - Defines column specifications and search strategy using background research output as input
column_definition._note_model: Recommended: claude-sonnet-4-5 (receives formatted research, no web search needed)
column_definition._note_max_tokens: Auto-tripled to 24000 when complete_enumeration detected with >20 entities (needs space to output all entities in complete_rows)
column_definition._subdomain_strategy: Use more subdomains (6-10) for challenging/niche topics or complex requirements. More subdomains = better coverage but more overlap. If authoritative lists found, structure subdomains as SEGMENTS of those lists (e.g., 'Countries A-E', 'NIH Awardees A-D')
column_definition._overshoot_note: Always target 30-50% more rows than user requested to ensure delivery after QC and deduplication

row_discovery._comment: Row discovery phase - Finds entities using progressive escalation strategy with optional global counter for early stopping
row_discovery._escalation_info: The escalation_strategy array defines progressive model fallback. Each level is tried until sufficient candidates found (based on min_candidates_percentage). This REPLACES the old 'model', 'web_search_model', 'scoring_model', 'progressive_context' settings

escalation_strategy.description: Primary: Premium search with sonar-pro for high quality results
escalation_strategy._note: Stop early if we get 75% of target_row_count (15/20 rows)

escalation_strategy.description: Fallback: Claude haiku with web search for comprehensive coverage
escalation_strategy._note: Moving to claude haiku for comprehensive coverage

escalation_strategy.description: Fallback: Claude Sonnet with web search for comprehensive coverage
escalation_strategy._note: No early stop - this is the final level, always runs if previous insufficient

row_discovery._global_counter_info: Global counter tracks total discovered rows across ALL subdomains and stops early if threshold reached. Prevents wasting API calls when we already have enough candidates globally
row_discovery._note_threshold: 100% means stop when total_discovered >= target_row_count. 120% would allow 20% overshooting
row_discovery._schema_validation_info: soft_schema=true allows LLM to omit optional fields in JSON responses (more flexible). false enforces strict schema adherence
row_discovery._note_multiplier: Deprecated - overshooting now controlled by subdomain target_rows from column_definition
row_discovery._note_parallel: null means sequential processing (safer for testing). Set to number >1 for parallel
row_discovery._deprecated_settings_info: These settings are NO LONGER USED - they're replaced by escalation_strategy
row_discovery._REMOVED_scoring_model: Was 'sonar-pro' - integrated scoring uses escalation_strategy model
row_discovery._REMOVED_integrated_scoring: Was true - always true now, not configurable
row_discovery._REMOVED_progressive_context: Was true - replaced by escalation_strategy with search_context_size
row_discovery._REMOVED_escalation_context_size: Was 'high' - concept replaced by multi-level escalation_strategy

qc_review._comment: QC layer reviews discovered rows and decides which to keep, reject, or reprioritize. Uses Claude Sonnet 4.5 without web search
qc_review._note_min_row_count: Minimum rows to guarantee after QC. If QC rejects too many, promotes rejected rows with highest qc_score to reach this minimum
qc_review._note_frontend_threshold: Below this threshold, frontend shows restart button to user
qc_review._note_qc_strategy: Options: 'traditional' (LLM-based QC review), 'validator' (validator lambda with 2x1 hard/soft weighting), 'both' (run both and merge - intersection=approve, union=demote)
qc_review._validator_qc_settings: Settings for validator lambda QC (used when qc_strategy='validator' or 'both')
qc_review._retrigger_settings: Retrigger allows QC to request additional discovery round with completely new subdomains if results insufficient
qc_review._note_retrigger: Typically 1 to prevent infinite loops. QC can analyze why results are poor and suggest new subdomains to try

table_population._comment: Table population phase - Fills in research columns for approved rows (not implemented in current v2.2 architecture)

config_generation._comment: Config generation phase - Creates validation config for the final table
config_generation._note_parallel: When true, config generation runs in parallel with row discovery to save time

validation._comment: Validation phase - Validates populated table data (legacy, not actively used in v2.2)

_DEPRECATED_SECTION._comment: This 'models' section is DEPRECATED and NO LONGER USED. Each phase now specifies its model directly in its own section
_DEPRECATED_SECTION._removed_reason: Phase-specific model settings (like interview.model, qc_review.model) take precedence and are actually used by the code

features._comment: Feature flags for optional table maker functionality
features._note_column_defs: Include column descriptions in CSV output
features._note_context_research: Allow user to specify context items for column definition to research
features._note_blue_circles: Frontend UI enhancement - show ID columns with special styling
features._note_independent: Use new independent row discovery architecture (v2.2+)

execution._comment: Overall execution settings for table generation pipeline
execution._note_steps: USER-VISIBLE: Step 1=Column Definition, Step 2=Row Discovery, Step 3=QC Review, Step 4=Finalization. INTERNAL Step 0=Background Research (not counted in user-facing progress)
execution._note_step_0: Background research runs as Step 0 (internal). User sees progress 0%->25% during this phase, then Step 1 starts at 25%
execution._restructure_caching: On restructure, background research is ALWAYS reused from cache (never re-run). Research is cached in conversation_state automatically.
execution._note_duration: Increased from 240s to account for background research (~30-60s) and optional table extraction (~20-40s)
execution._note_parallel_step2_deprecated: Deprecated - config_generation still runs in parallel with row discovery, but background_research must complete before column_definition

_REMOVED_SETTINGS_SUMMARY._comment: Documentation of what was removed and why

row_discovery.model.removed_reason: Replaced by escalation_strategy array which allows progressive fallback

row_discovery.web_search_model.removed_reason: Was redundant with 'model' setting. Integrated scoring uses the model from escalation_strategy
row_discovery.web_search_model.migration: Implicit in escalation_strategy - each level defines its own model

row_discovery.scoring_model.removed_reason: Integrated scoring is now always enabled and uses the model from escalation_strategy
row_discovery.scoring_model.migration: No action needed - integrated scoring automatic

row_discovery.integrated_scoring.removed_reason: Always true in v2.2+. Discovery and scoring happen in single API call for efficiency
row_discovery.integrated_scoring.migration: No configuration needed - it's always integrated

row_discovery.progressive_context.removed_reason: Replaced by more flexible escalation_strategy with explicit context sizes per level

row_discovery.escalation_context_size.removed_reason: Old 2-level system replaced by N-level escalation_strategy
row_discovery.escalation_context_size.migration: Set context in each escalation_strategy level as needed

models.removed_reason: Redundant with phase-specific model settings. Code reads from interview.model, qc_review.model, etc.
models.migration: Use phase-specific settings (interview.model instead of models.interview)
